1.gl: properly, to creak (or emit a stridulous sound), i.e. to shout (usually for joy)
1.kj: aloud for joy, cry out, be joyful (greatly, make to) rejoice, (cause to) shout (for joy), (cause to) sing (aloud, for joy, out), triumph.
convenient, equity, Jasher, just, meet(-est), [phrase] pleased well right(-eous), straight, (most) upright(-ly, -ness).

1.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
1.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

2.gl: physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)
2.dv: a primitive root; used only as denominative from H3027 (יָד); literally, to use (i.e. hold out) the hand;

2.gl: play upon it; to make music, accompanied by the voice; hence to celebrate in song and music
2.kj: give praise, sing forth praises, psalms.
2.dv: a primitive root (perhaps identical with H2168 (זָמַר) through the idea of striking with the fingers); properly, to touch the strings or parts of a mu

2.gl: a skin-bag for liquids (from collapsing when empty); hence, a vase (as similar in shape when full); also a lyre (as having a body of like form)
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
2.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

2.gl: ten; by abbreviated form ten strings, and so a decachord

2.dv: from a unused root meaning to twang;

3.gl: to be (causative) make well, literally (sound, beautiful) or figuratively (happy, successful, right)

3.gl: properly, to thrum, i.e. beat atune with the fingers; expectation. to play on astringed instrument; hence (generally), to make music
3.kj: player on instruments, sing to the stringed instruments, melody, ministrel, play(-er, -ing).

3.gl: clamor, i.e. acclamation of joy or a battle-cry; especially clangorof trumpets, as an alarum
3.kj: alarm, blow(-ing) (of, the) (trumpets), joy, jubile, loud noise, rejoicing, shout(-ing), (high, joyful) sound(-ing).
behold (by mistake for H7789 (שׁוּר)), sing(-er, -ing man, -ing woman).
3.dv: or (the original form) שׁוּר; (1 Samuel 18:6), a primitive root (rather identical with H7788 (שׁוּר) through the idea of strolling minstrelsy);

convenient, equity, Jasher, just, meet(-est), [phrase] pleased well right(-eous), straight, (most) upright(-ly, -ness).
